ddls:
CREATE TABLE "houses" (
  "house_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each house */,
  "house_name" TEXT /* Name of the house */,
  "house_description" TEXT /* Description of the house */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("house_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "house_id" INTEGER /* ID of the house where the session was held */,
  "term" INTEGER /* Term of the session */,
  "session_date" TEXT /* Date of the session */,
  "meeting" INTEGER /* Meeting number */,
  "sitting" INTEGER /* Sitting number */,
  "session_status" TEXT /* Status of the session (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speakers" (
  "speaker_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each speaker */,
  "speaker_name" TEXT /* Name of the speaker */,
  "speaker_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the speaker */,
  "speaker_birth" TEXT /* Birth date of the speaker */,
  "speaker_bio" TEXT /* Biography of the speaker */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("speaker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speaker_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speaker_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the type */,
  "type_description" TEXT /* Description of the type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speaker_role_assignments" (
  "speaker_id" INTEGER /* ID of the speaker */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date when the speaker was assigned the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("speaker_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speaker_type_assignments" (
  "speaker_id" INTEGER /* ID of the speaker */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the type */,
  "assignment_date" TEXT /* Date when the speaker was assigned the type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("speaker_id", "type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parties" (
  "party_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each party */,
  "party_name" TEXT /* Name of the party */,
  "party_status" TEXT /* Status of the party (e.g., government, opposition) */,
  "party_description" TEXT /* Description of the party */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("party_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speaker_party_affiliations" (
  "speaker_id" INTEGER /* ID of the speaker */,
  "party_id" INTEGER /* ID of the party */,
  "affiliation_date" TEXT /* Date when the speaker joined the party */,
  "affiliation_status" TEXT /* Status of the affiliation (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("speaker_id", "party_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "speeches" (
  "speech_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each speech */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* ID of the session where the speech was made */,
  "speaker_id" INTEGER /* ID of the speaker who made the speech */,
  "speech_date" TEXT /* Date when the speech was made */,
  "transcript" TEXT /* Transcript of the speech */,
  "speech_status" TEXT /* Status of the speech (e.g., draft, published) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("speech_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_speeches_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_speeches_speaker_id FOREIGN KEY ("speaker_id") REFERENCES speakers ("speaker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: ID of the house where the session was held
Status of the session (e.g., active, inactive)
Date when the speaker was assigned the role
Date when the speaker was assigned the type
Status of the party (e.g., government, opposition)
Date when the speaker joined the party
Status of the affiliation (e.g., active, inactive)
ID of the session where the speech was made
ID of the speaker who made the speech
Date when the speech was made
Status of the speech (e.g., draft, published)
Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin)
